statement: The duplicate charge is a check-then-act (TOCTOU) race on the idempotency cache: lookup() and reserve() are two non-atomic operations with no per-key mutual exclusion, so two concurrent requests with the same key both observe MISS and both proceed to charge.
because: The boundary evidence shows both lookup() calls for ik_3af91c complete and return MISS (t=1.40ms, 1.55ms) before either reserve() writes (t=3.55ms, 3.70ms). The working inventory path that avoids this uses one atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT instead.
single_variable: presence/absence of a per-key critical section spanning lookup..reserve. Nothing else will be changed by the test.
prediction_if_true: If I serialize the two requests through a single per-key lock around lookup+reserve (test-only), the duplicate rate drops to 0% at every concurrency level, with no other code touched.
would_falsify: If duplicates persist even with a correct per-key lock held across lookup..reserve, then the race is NOT the (sole) cause - look elsewhere (e.g. PSP retry, or reserve() writing two rows for another reason).
source: systematic-debugging Phase 3.1 'Form Single Hypothesis' (state X is root cause because Y; be specific; write the kill condition)
